name: getSmallestMediaQuery
description:
Finds the media query that applies to the smallest viewport.
Prioritizes `max-width` values when available; otherwise, falls back to `min-width`.

params.name: queries
params.description: An array of valid CSS media query strings.

return.description: The smallest media query or `null` if none are valid.

name: getBackgroundImageProps
description: Generates background image properties for different breakpoints.

params.name: options
params.description: An array of background image options.

return.description: An object mapping breakpoints to their corresponding media query and image properties.

name: Style
description: Generates and applies responsive background image styles. Only supported in React 19.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The id of the html element, for generating the responsive styles.

fields.name: href
fields.description: A unique id for the <style> element. Allows React to de-duplicate styles that have the same href.

fields.name: bgImageProps
fields.description: An object mapping breakpoints to their corresponding media query and image properties.

return.description: A `<style>` element containing the computed background image CSS rules.

name: BackgroundImage
description: Renders a background image component with support for responsive images and optional preloading.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique id for the background image html element.

fields.name: as
fields.description: The HTML tag or React component to use as the wrapper. Defaults to `<div>`.

fields.name: preload
fields.description:
Optional prop for preloading the background image. This works similar to the `priority` prop on the Next.js image.
Should be used for any `<BackgroundImage />` component that is above the fold, and flagged as the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).
remarks:
[important] For preloading to work properly, media queries on `<link rel="preload">` elements cannot overlap.
Media queries on `<link rel="preload">` elements do not function the way they do on HTML elements.
The user agent will look at multiple `<link rel="preload">` media queries and find multiple matches if there is any overlap in the media queries.
This can lead to performance issues, where multiple images are preloaded.

To avoid this, the `<BackgroundImage />` component will automatically adjust the media queries set on the preload links, by adding or subtracting 1 px.
If this functionality causes any issues, it can be disabled with the `normalizeMediaQueries` prop.

fields.name: images
fields.description: An array of image objects of the `BackgroundImageOptions` type.

fields.name: className
fields.description: Optional prop for any CSS class name(s) for the background image element.

fields.name: style
fields.description: Optional prop for any CSS style properties for the background image element.

name: parseMediaQuery
description: Parses a media query to get it's min and max values as numbers.

params.name: media
params.description: The media query string to parse.

return.description: An object with min and max values.

name: getFallbackMediaQuery
description: Generate a fallback media query covering everything below the smallest min-width.

return.description: A fallback media query item or null.

name: getMediaQueries
description:
Normalize media queries to remove overlapping ranges:
- Bump first source's min-width by 1 to prevent overlap with fallback.
- For adjacent sources, reduce max-width by 1 if it overlaps the next min-width.

fields.name: normalize
fields.description: Enables or disables media query normalization to remove overlaps. Defaults to `true`.

fields.name: fallback
fields.description: Optional fallback media query.

return.description: Normalized object of objects, keyed by uuid.
